{{short description|Canadian social psychologist}}

'''Lara Beth Aknin''' is a Canadian professor of social psychology at [[Simon Fraser University]], and associate editor of the [[World Happiness Report]].<ref>{{cite web |date=March 19, 2021 |title=Report assesses impact of COVID-19 on global happiness |url=http://www.sfu.ca/sfunews/stories/2021/03/report-assesses-impact-of-covid-19-on-global-happiness.html |access-date=March 19, 2021 |website=sfu.ca}}</ref>

==Career==
After earning her PhD in social psychology from the [[University of British Columbia]], Aknin joined the faculty of psychology at [[Simon Fraser University]] in 2012.<ref name = "x">{{cite web |title=Generous spending leads to increased happiness, concludes SFU psychologist Lara Aknin in World Happiness Report |url=https://www.sfu.ca/fass/news/2019/03/generous-spending-increased-happiness-lara-aknin.html |website=sfu.ca |access-date=November 12, 2019 |date=March 29, 2019}}</ref> That year, she published "''Giving Leads to Happiness in Young Children''" with J. Kiley Hamlin and Elizabeth Dunn, which supported the idea that humans may have evolved to find giving rewarding.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Enayati |first1=Amanda |title=For kids, it's better to give than receive |url=https://www.cnn.com/2012/09/17/living/giving-makes-children-happy/index.html |website=cnn.ca |access-date=November 12, 2019 |date=September 17, 2012}}</ref>

In 2014, Aknin, [[Michael Norton (professor)|Michael Norton]] and [[Elizabeth Dunn]] co-published a [[Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council]] (SSHRC) and CIHR funded review of whether spending money had a positive effect on people's happiness. Her research has shown that people often experience greater happiness when they spend money on others, rather then themselves. <ref>{{cite web |title=Looking to increase your sense of abundance? Research by Dr. Lara Aknin has established that giving to others can increase feelings of well-being |url=https://www.sfu.ca/vpresearch/Research50/abundance.html |website=sfu.ca |access-date=November 12, 2019 }}{{Dead link|date=July 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> The following year, her contributions to the field of social psychology earned her the President's New Researcher Award from the [[Canadian Psychological Association]]<ref>{{cite web |title=2014–2015 Annual Performance Report |url=https://www.cifar.ca/docs/default-source/accountability/capr-2014-2015-final-english.pdf?sfvrsn=a557929d_2 |website=cifar.ca |access-date=November 12, 2019 |page=72 |archive-date=July 2, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200702112917/https://www.cifar.ca/docs/default-source/accountability/capr-2014-2015-final-english.pdf?sfvrsn=a557929d_2 }}</ref> and a fellowship at the [[Canadian Institute for Advanced Research]].<ref name = "x"/> By 2019, she received a SSHRC grant for her project, "Can Repeated and Reflective Giving Nurture Canada's Next Generation of Philanthropists?"<ref>{{cite web |title=Lara Aknin awarded SSHRC Partnership Engage grant for nurturing the next generation of philanthropists |url=https://www.sfu.ca/psychology/news/LaraAkninAwardedSSHRCGrant.html |website=sfu.ca |access-date=November 12, 2019 |date=October 15, 2019 |archive-date=February 19, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200219211357/http://www.sfu.ca/psychology/news/LaraAkninAwardedSSHRCGrant.html }}</ref> She was also honoured by the university for her research and contributions to social Psychology with the title "Distinguished SFU Professor."<ref>{{cite web |title=Simon Fraser University honours our inaugural Distinguished SFU Professors |url=https://www.sfu.ca/sfunews/stories/2019/09/simon-fraser-university-honours-our-inaugural-distinguished-sfu-.html |website=sfu.ca |access-date=November 12, 2019 |date=September 19, 2019}}</ref>

During the [[COVID-19 pandemic]], she served as chair of the [[Mental Health and Wellbeing Task Force of the Lancet COVID-19 Commission]].<ref>{{cite journal |title=Lancet COVID-19 Commission Statement on the occasion of the 75th session of the UN General Assembly |journal=The Lancet |date=September 14, 2020 |volume=396 |issue=10257 |url=https://www.thelancet.com/article/S0140-6736(20)31927-9/fulltext |access-date=March 19, 2021}}</ref> 

==Publications==
{{Scholia|Q74770207}}

* {{cite journal |last1=Dunn |first1=Elizabeth W. |last2=Aknin |first2=Lara B. |last3=Norton |first3=Michael I. |title=Spending Money on Others Promotes Happiness |journal=Science |date=21 March 2008 |volume=319 |issue=5870 |pages=1687–1688 |doi=10.1126/science.1150952 |pmid=18356530 |bibcode=2008Sci...319.1687D |s2cid=29589887 |url=https://www.science.org/doi/abs/10.1126/science.1150952 |language=EN|url-access=subscription }}
* {{cite journal |last1=Aknin |first1=Lara B. |last2=Barrington-Leigh |first2=Christopher P. |last3=Dunn |first3=Elizabeth W. |last4=Helliwell |first4=John F. |author-link4=John F. Helliwell |last5=Burns |first5=Justine |author-link5=Justine Burns |last6=Biswas-Diener |first6=Robert |author-link6=Robert Biswas-Diener |last7=Kemeza |first7=Imelda |last8=Nyende |first8=Paul |last9=Ashton-James |first9=Claire E. |last10=Norton |first10=Michael I. |title=Prosocial spending and well-being: Cross-cultural evidence for a psychological universal. |journal=Journal of Personality and Social Psychology |date=April 2013 |volume=104 |issue=4 |pages=635–652 |doi=10.1037/a0031578 |pmid=23421360 |s2cid=5403883 |url=https://psycnet.apa.org/doiLanding?doi=10.1037/a0031578 |language=en |issn=1939-1315|hdl=11370/24a6ab33-643b-47af-9e5b-4110f51a55bb |hdl-access=free }}

* {{cite journal |last1=Aknin |first1=Lara B. |last2=Norton |first2=Michael I. |last3=Dunn |first3=Elizabeth W. |title=From wealth to well-being? Money matters, but less than people think |journal=The Journal of Positive Psychology |date=1 November 2009 |volume=4 |issue=6 |pages=523–527 |doi=10.1080/17439760903271421 |s2cid=9911004 |url=https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/17439760903271421 |issn=1743-9760|url-access=subscription }}

* {{cite journal |last1=Aknin |first1=Lara B. |last2=Broesch |first2=Tanya |last3=Hamlin |first3=J. Kiley |last4=Van de Vondervoort |first4=Julia W. |title=Prosocial behavior leads to happiness in a small-scale rural society. |journal=Journal of Experimental Psychology: General |date=August 2015 |volume=144 |issue=4 |pages=788–795 |doi=10.1037/xge0000082 |pmid=26030168 |url=https://psycnet.apa.org/getdoi.cfm?doi=10.1037/xge0000082 |language=en |issn=1939-2222|url-access=subscription }}
